original_hypothesis.title: Front-runnable setDrawManager Allows Complete Protocol Takeover
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `setDrawManager` function lacks access control and can be called by anyone if the `drawManager` is currently `address(0)`. If the contract is deployed without a draw manager, an attacker can front-run the initialization transaction to take over the role.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The draw manager controls draw closures and can withdraw the entire reserve. Taking over this role compromises the entire prize pool. While dependent on deployment configuration, it is a critical vulnerability if triggered.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: PrizePool.sol constructor allows `params.drawManager` to be zero.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `function setDrawManager(address _drawManager) external` has no access control other than checking if it's currently zero.

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker calls `setDrawManager(attacker)`. Attacker then calls `withdrawReserve(attacker, reserve)`.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker steals all reserve funds and controls draw outcomes.

validation_requirements: Check deployment scripts for drawManager initialization

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
